Data and group coursework

SMM048 (2025-26) Group Coursework 01 - Group 07

Ardi Wira Sudarmo

Bong Su Kil

Yanwun Mak

Yeng Issah

04 3월 2026

1 Factor Analysis

## R Markdown

data1 <- read.table("dataset1.txt", header = TRUE)
sum(is.na(data1))
## [1] 508
colSums(is.na(data1)) 
##  X1  X2  X3  X4  X5  X6  X7  X8  X9 X10 X11 X12 X13 X14 X15 X16 X17 X18 X19 X20 
##  16  27  26  19  16  21  24  20  26  16  23  16  25   9  21  22  21  11  36  29 
## X21 X22 X23 X24 X25 
##  22   0  28  14  20
data1_clean <- na.omit(data1)

var_label(data1_clean$X1)  <- "Be indifferent to the feelings of others"
var_label(data1_clean$X2)  <- "Inquire about others' well-being"
var_label(data1_clean$X3)  <- "Know how to comfort others"
var_label(data1_clean$X4)  <- "Love children"
var_label(data1_clean$X5)  <- "Make people feel at ease"
var_label(data1_clean$X6)  <- "Be exacting in my work"
var_label(data1_clean$X7)  <- "Continue until everything is perfect"
var_label(data1_clean$X8)  <- "Do things according to a plan"
var_label(data1_clean$X9)  <- "Do things in a half-way manner"
var_label(data1_clean$X10) <- "Waste my time"
var_label(data1_clean$X11) <- "Don't talk a lot"
var_label(data1_clean$X12) <- "Find it difficult to approach others"
var_label(data1_clean$X13) <- "Know how to captivate people"
var_label(data1_clean$X14) <- "Make friends easily"
var_label(data1_clean$X15) <- "Take charge"
var_label(data1_clean$X16) <- "Get angry easily"
var_label(data1_clean$X17) <- "Get irritated easily"
var_label(data1_clean$X18) <- "Have frequent mood swings"
var_label(data1_clean$X19) <- "Often feel blue"
var_label(data1_clean$X20) <- "Panic easily"
var_label(data1_clean$X21) <- "Be full of ideas"
var_label(data1_clean$X22) <- "Avoid difficult reading material"
var_label(data1_clean$X23) <- "Carry the conversation to a higher level"
var_label(data1_clean$X24) <- "Spend time reflecting on things"
var_label(data1_clean$X25) <- "Will not probe deeply into a subject"

An exploratory factor analysis (EFA) was conducted on the 25 personality items measured on a 6-point Likert scale. The dataset contained some missing value(NA), which are handled using deletion. In odrer to determine the number of factors, two methods(PCA vs FA) were compared to improve explicity.

summary(data1_clean)
##        X1              X2              X3              X4       
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:4.000  
##  Median :2.000   Median :5.000   Median :5.000   Median :5.000  
##  Mean   :2.406   Mean   :4.797   Mean   :4.599   Mean   :4.688  
##  3rd Qu.:3.000   3rd Qu.:6.000   3rd Qu.:6.000   3rd Qu.:6.000  
##  Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000  
##        X5              X6              X7              X8            X9      
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.0   Min.   :1.00  
##  1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:4.0   1st Qu.:1.00  
##  Median :5.000   Median :5.000   Median :5.000   Median :5.0   Median :2.00  
##  Mean   :4.544   Mean   :4.525   Mean   :4.372   Mean   :4.3   Mean   :2.55  
##  3rd Qu.:5.000   3rd Qu.:5.000   3rd Qu.:5.000   3rd Qu.:5.0   3rd Qu.:4.00  
##  Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.0   Max.   :6.00  
##       X10             X11             X12             X13       
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:3.000  
##  Median :3.000   Median :3.000   Median :3.000   Median :4.000  
##  Mean   :3.306   Mean   :2.979   Mean   :3.154   Mean   :3.984  
##  3rd Qu.:5.000   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:5.000  
##  Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000  
##       X14             X15             X16             X17       
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000  
##  Median :5.000   Median :5.000   Median :3.000   Median :4.000  
##  Mean   :4.409   Mean   :4.391   Mean   :2.944   Mean   :3.518  
##  3rd Qu.:6.000   3rd Qu.:5.000   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:5.000  
##  Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000  
##       X18             X19             X20             X21       
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:4.000  
##  Median :3.000   Median :3.000   Median :3.000   Median :5.000  
##  Mean   :3.225   Mean   :3.202   Mean   :2.971   Mean   :4.813  
##  3rd Qu.:5.000   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:6.000  
##  Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000  
##       X22             X23            X24             X25       
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.00   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.:4.00   1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:1.000  
##  Median :2.000   Median :5.00   Median :5.000   Median :2.000  
##  Mean   :2.685   Mean   :4.45   Mean   :4.925   Mean   :2.469  
##  3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:5.00   3rd Qu.:6.000   3rd Qu.:3.000  
##  Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.00   Max.   :6.000   Max.   :6.000
data1.pr <- prcomp(data1_clean, rotate="none")
summary(data1.pr)
## Importance of components:
##                           PC1    PC2     PC3     PC4     PC5     PC6     PC7
## Standard deviation     3.2916 2.4515 2.03039 1.88148 1.75299 1.45470 1.34398
## Proportion of Variance 0.2157 0.1196 0.08205 0.07046 0.06116 0.04212 0.03595
## Cumulative Proportion  0.2157 0.3353 0.41732 0.48778 0.54894 0.59106 0.62701
##                            PC8     PC9    PC10    PC11    PC12    PC13    PC14
## Standard deviation     1.31927 1.21439 1.19283 1.15951 1.09845 1.08721 1.06148
## Proportion of Variance 0.03464 0.02935 0.02832 0.02676 0.02402 0.02353 0.02243
## Cumulative Proportion  0.66165 0.69100 0.71932 0.74608 0.77010 0.79362 0.81605
##                           PC15    PC16    PC17    PC18    PC19    PC20    PC21
## Standard deviation     1.03899 0.99463 0.98610 0.95306 0.93604 0.91107 0.90039
## Proportion of Variance 0.02149 0.01969 0.01935 0.01808 0.01744 0.01652 0.01614
## Cumulative Proportion  0.83753 0.85722 0.87658 0.89466 0.91210 0.92862 0.94475
##                          PC22    PC23    PC24    PC25
## Standard deviation     0.8680 0.85904 0.82124 0.78103
## Proportion of Variance 0.0150 0.01469 0.01342 0.01214
## Cumulative Proportion  0.9597 0.97444 0.98786 1.00000
pr.var = data1.pr$sdev^2
pve=pr.var/sum(pr.var)

plot(
  pve,
  xlab = "principal Component",
  ylab = "Proportion of Variance Explained",
  ylim = c(0, 1),
  type = "b"
)

plot(
  cumsum(pve),
  xlab = "principal Component",
  ylab = "Cummulative Proportion of Variance Explained",
  ylim = c(0, 1),
  type = "b"
)

cumsum(pve)
##  [1] 0.2156497 0.3352694 0.4173207 0.4877777 0.5489400 0.5910586 0.6270095
##  [8] 0.6616506 0.6910031 0.7193226 0.7460817 0.7700968 0.7936231 0.8160488
## [15] 0.8375344 0.8572246 0.8765783 0.8946568 0.9120955 0.9286164 0.9447520
## [22] 0.9597478 0.9744354 0.9878589 1.0000000
en <- eigen(cor(data1_clean))
en$values
##  [1] 5.1343112 2.7518867 2.1427020 1.8523276 1.5481628 1.0735825 0.8395389
##  [8] 0.7992062 0.7189892 0.6880888 0.6763734 0.6517998 0.6232530 0.5965628
## [15] 0.5630908 0.5433053 0.5145175 0.4945031 0.4826395 0.4489210 0.4233661
## [22] 0.4006715 0.3878045 0.3818568 0.2625390
plot(en$values, type = "o")

fa.parallel(
  data1_clean,
  fa = "fa",
  main = "Factor Analysis"
)

## Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  6  and the number of components =  NA
result <- factanal(data1_clean, factors = 6, rotation="varimax", scores="regression")

result
## 
## Call:
## factanal(x = data1_clean, factors = 6, scores = "regression",     rotation = "varimax")
## 
## Uniquenesses:
##    X1    X2    X3    X4    X5    X6    X7    X8    X9   X10   X11   X12   X13 
## 0.675 0.482 0.473 0.696 0.516 0.637 0.498 0.685 0.424 0.566 0.613 0.453 0.522 
##   X14   X15   X16   X17   X18   X19   X20   X21   X22   X23   X24   X25 
## 0.432 0.599 0.273 0.302 0.480 0.504 0.652 0.663 0.703 0.510 0.756 0.640 
## 
## Loadings:
##     Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5 Factor6
## X1   0.106                  -0.530  -0.105   0.135 
## X2           0.239   0.128   0.663                 
## X3           0.359   0.122   0.601           0.143 
## X4           0.231   0.243   0.401  -0.129         
## X5  -0.142   0.435   0.107   0.461           0.224 
## X6                   0.557           0.189         
## X7                   0.677                   0.160 
## X8                   0.548   0.107                 
## X9   0.219          -0.638  -0.105  -0.133   0.300 
## X10  0.278  -0.182  -0.546                   0.143 
## X11         -0.583          -0.120           0.156 
## X12  0.239  -0.674          -0.110           0.114 
## X13          0.566   0.102   0.170   0.251   0.237 
## X14 -0.136   0.674   0.115   0.222  -0.107   0.148 
## X15          0.506   0.303           0.207         
## X16  0.819                  -0.167          -0.125 
## X17  0.805                  -0.128          -0.176 
## X18  0.710                                         
## X19  0.561  -0.344  -0.162                   0.181 
## X20  0.512  -0.163                  -0.158   0.165 
## X21          0.234   0.127           0.483   0.181 
## X22  0.158                          -0.494   0.137 
## X23          0.337                   0.572   0.193 
## X24  0.205  -0.169           0.133   0.352   0.177 
## X25                                 -0.572   0.146 
## 
##                Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5 Factor6
## SS loadings      2.722   2.652   2.075   1.672   1.512   0.614
## Proportion Var   0.109   0.106   0.083   0.067   0.060   0.025
## Cumulative Var   0.109   0.215   0.298   0.365   0.425   0.450
## 
## Test of the hypothesis that 6 factors are sufficient.
## The chi square statistic is 896.7 on 165 degrees of freedom.
## The p-value is 5.73e-101
print(result, digits=2, cutoff=0.5)
## 
## Call:
## factanal(x = data1_clean, factors = 6, scores = "regression",     rotation = "varimax")
## 
## Uniquenesses:
##   X1   X2   X3   X4   X5   X6   X7   X8   X9  X10  X11  X12  X13  X14  X15  X16 
## 0.67 0.48 0.47 0.70 0.52 0.64 0.50 0.69 0.42 0.57 0.61 0.45 0.52 0.43 0.60 0.27 
##  X17  X18  X19  X20  X21  X22  X23  X24  X25 
## 0.30 0.48 0.50 0.65 0.66 0.70 0.51 0.76 0.64 
## 
## Loadings:
##     Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5 Factor6
## X1                          -0.53                  
## X2                           0.66                  
## X3                           0.60                  
## X4                                                 
## X5                                                 
## X6                   0.56                          
## X7                   0.68                          
## X8                   0.55                          
## X9                  -0.64                          
## X10                 -0.55                          
## X11         -0.58                                  
## X12         -0.67                                  
## X13          0.57                                  
## X14          0.67                                  
## X15          0.51                                  
## X16  0.82                                          
## X17  0.81                                          
## X18  0.71                                          
## X19  0.56                                          
## X20  0.51                                          
## X21                                                
## X22                                                
## X23                                  0.57          
## X24                                                
## X25                                 -0.57          
## 
##                Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5 Factor6
## SS loadings       2.72    2.65    2.07    1.67    1.51    0.61
## Proportion Var    0.11    0.11    0.08    0.07    0.06    0.02
## Cumulative Var    0.11    0.21    0.30    0.36    0.43    0.45
## 
## Test of the hypothesis that 6 factors are sufficient.
## The chi square statistic is 896.7 on 165 degrees of freedom.
## The p-value is 5.73e-101
print(result$loadings, cutoff=0)
## 
## Loadings:
##     Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5 Factor6

## Proportion Var   0.109   0.106   0.083   0.067   0.060   0.025
## Cumulative Var   0.109   0.215   0.298   0.365   0.425   0.450
result2 <- factanal(data1_clean, factors = 5, rotation="varimax", scores="regression")
result2
## 
## Call:
## factanal(x = data1_clean, factors = 5, scores = "regression",     rotation = "varimax")
## 
## Uniquenesses:
##    X1    X2    X3    X4    X5    X6    X7    X8    X9   X10   X11   X12   X13 
## 0.830 0.576 0.466 0.691 0.512 0.660 0.569 0.677 0.510 0.557 0.634 0.454 0.558 
##   X14   X15   X16   X17   X18   X19   X20   X21   X22   X23   X24   X25 
## 0.468 0.592 0.271 0.337 0.478 0.507 0.664 0.675 0.744 0.518 0.752 0.726 
## 
## Loadings:
##     Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5
## X1   0.104                  -0.393         
## X2           0.191   0.144   0.601         
## X3           0.280   0.110   0.662         
## X4           0.181   0.234   0.454  -0.109 
## X5  -0.124   0.351           0.580         
## X6                   0.533           0.221 
## X7                   0.624   0.127   0.140 
## X8                   0.554   0.122         
## X9   0.218          -0.653                 
## X10  0.272  -0.190  -0.573                 
## X11         -0.587          -0.120         
## X12  0.233  -0.674  -0.106  -0.151         
## X13          0.490           0.315   0.313 
## X14 -0.121   0.613           0.363         
## X15          0.491   0.310   0.120   0.234 
## X16  0.816                  -0.214         
## X17  0.787                  -0.202         
## X18  0.714                                 
## X19  0.562  -0.367  -0.192                 
## X20  0.518  -0.187           0.106  -0.137 
## X21          0.182   0.103           0.524 
## X22  0.163          -0.113   0.102  -0.454 
## X23          0.276           0.153   0.614 
## X24  0.207  -0.220           0.144   0.368 
## X25                                 -0.512

##                Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5
## SS loadings      2.687   2.320   2.034   1.978   1.557
## Proportion Var   0.107   0.093   0.081   0.079   0.062
## Cumulative Var   0.107   0.200   0.282   0.361   0.423
## 
## Test of the hypothesis that 5 factors are sufficient.
## The chi square statistic is 1490.59 on 185 degrees of freedom.
## The p-value is 1.22e-202
print(result2, digits=2, cutoff=0.5)
## 
## Call:
## factanal(x = data1_clean, factors = 5, scores = "regression",     rotation = "varimax")

## Uniquenesses:

##     Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5

print(result2$loadings, cutoff=0)

##     Factor1 Factor2 Factor3 Factor4 Factor5

## SS loadings      2.687   2.320   2.034   1.978   1.557
## Proportion Var   0.107   0.093   0.081   0.079   0.062
## Cumulative Var   0.107   0.200   0.282   0.361   0.423

2 copula analysis

library(insuranceData)
library(GJRM)

data(dataCar)
names(dataCar)
##  [1] "veh_value" "exposure"  "clm"       "numclaims" "claimcst0" "veh_body" 
##  [7] "veh_age"   "gender"    "area"      "agecat"    "X_OBSTAT_"
num_data2 <- dataCar$numclaims
cost_data2 <- dataCar$claimcst0
# option1 num ~ poisson
#cs2 cw1 q2 copula

data_cop <- subset(dataCar, numclaims > 0 & claimcst0 > 0 & is.finite(claimcst0))
eq1 <- numclaims ~ 1
eq2 <- claimcst0 ~ 1

#claim frequency distribution
#use offset since exposure is not constant, since glm uses log link function so offset = log(exposure)
#truncated frequency
tpo <- gamlss(list(eq1), family = "tP", data = data_cop, offset = log(data_cop$exposure))
tnbi <- gamlss(list(eq1), family = "tNBI", data = data_cop, offset = log(data_cop$exposure))

print(AIC(tpo,tnbi))
##      df      AIC
## tpo   1 2283.909
## tnbi  2 2282.460
print(BIC(tpo,tnbi))
##      df      BIC
## tpo   1 2290.348
## tnbi  2 2295.338
summary(tnbi)
## 
## Distribution: Truncated Negative Binomial - Type I
## 
## EQUATION 1
## Link function for mu: log 
## Formula: numclaims ~ 1
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   -2.193      0.620  -3.537 0.000405 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## sigma = 0.987(0.138,11.5)
## n = 4624  total edf = 2
conv.check(tnbi)
## 
## Maximum absolute gradient value: 2.435232e-07
## Observed information matrix is positive definite
res.check(tnbi)

summary(tpo)
## 
## Distribution: Truncated Poisson
## 
## EQUATION 1
## Link function for mu: log 
## Formula: numclaims ~ 1
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept) -1.53312    0.05582  -27.46   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## n = 4624  total edf = 1
conv.check(tpo)
## 
## Maximum absolute gradient value: 2.137928e-05
## Observed information matrix is positive definite
res.check(tpo)

#claim severity distribution
ln <- gamlss(list(eq2), family = "LN", data = data_cop)
ga <- gamlss(list(eq2), family = "GA", data = data_cop)

AIC(ln, ga)
BIC(ln, ga)
summary(ln)
## 
## Distribution: Log-normal
## 
## EQUATION 1
## Link function for mu: log 
## Formula: claimcst0 ~ 1
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)  6.81008    0.01749   389.4   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## sigma = 1.19(1.16,1.21)
## n = 4624  total edf = 2
conv.check(ln)
## 
## Maximum absolute gradient value: 1.116845e-09
## Observed information matrix is positive definite
res.check(ln)

summary(ga)
## 
## Distribution: Gamma
## 
## EQUATION 1
## Link function for mu: log 
## Formula: claimcst0 ~ 1
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)  7.60808    0.01698   448.1   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## sigma = 1.15(1.14,1.17)
## n = 4624  total edf = 2
conv.check(ga)
## 
## Maximum absolute gradient value: 2.72951e-11
## Observed information matrix is positive definite
res.check(ga)

3 principal component Analysis

## R Markdown

data3 <- read.table("dataset3.txt", header = TRUE)
data3 <- na.omit(data3)

pr.out <- prcomp(data3, scale.  = TRUE)

pr.var <- pr.out$sdev^2
pve <- pr.var / sum(pr.var)

corrplot(cor(data3), method = "color", type = "upper",
         tl.cex = 0.7, number.cex = 0.7)

ggplot(data.frame(PC = 1:length(pve), PVE = pve), aes(x = PC, y = PVE)) +
  geom_line(color = "red", size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(PVE, 2)),
    vjust = -0.8,
    color = "black",
    size = 4
  ) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(title = "Scree Plot with PVE", x = "Principal Component", y = "Proportion of Variance Explained") +
  theme_minimal()

cum_pve <- cumsum(pve)

ggplot(data.frame(PC = 1:length(cum_pve), CUM = cum_pve), aes(x = PC, y = CUM)) +
  geom_line(color = "red", size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5,
             linetype = "dashed",
             color = "red") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(CUM, 2)),
    vjust = -0.8,
    color = "black",
    size = 4
  ) +
  ylim(0, 1.1) +
  labs(title = "Cumulative Variance Explained", x = "Principal Component", y = "Cumulative PVE") +
  theme_minimal()

fa.parallel(
  data3,
  fa = "both",
  main = "Parallel Analysis for PCA, FA"
)

## Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  3  and the number of components =  3
scores <- data.frame(pr.out$x)

# ggplot(scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2)) +
#   geom_point(color = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
#   labs(title = "PCA Score Plot (PC1 vs PC2)", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
#   theme_minimal()

round(pr.out$rotation[,1:3], 2)
##                               PC1   PC2   PC3
## Income                      -0.38  0.48 -0.24
## Age                          0.33  0.57 -0.29
## Spending_Score              -0.48 -0.27  0.12
## Shopping_Frequency          -0.51  0.13 -0.07
## Brand_Loyalty               -0.50  0.15 -0.08
## Website_Visits              -0.04 -0.13  0.09
## Social_Media_Engagement     -0.02 -0.10 -0.57
## Email_Open_Rate              0.02  0.50  0.42
## Customer_Satisfaction_Score  0.01 -0.17 -0.09
## Promotions_Utilized         -0.06  0.12  0.56
## Cart_Abandonment_Rate        0.02 -0.09 -0.03
load <- pr.out$rotation[, 1:3]
df <- melt(load)

ggplot(df, aes(Var2, Var1, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white") +
  labs(title = "PCA Loadings Heatmap (PC1~PC5)",
       x = "Principal Components", y = "Variables") +
  theme_minimal()

km <- kmeans(data3, centers = 3)
scores2 <- data.frame(pr.out$x[,1:2], cluster = factor(km$cluster))

ggplot(scores2, aes(PC1, PC2, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal()
